{"query":"Perplexity Model Council parallel frontier models GPT-5.4 Claude Opus 4.6 Gemini 3.1 Pro synthesis feature","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1705,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.perplexity.ai/changelog/what-we-shipped---march-6-2026","title":"Perplexity Changelog","content":"Teach Perplexity Computer how to handle any task once, and it remembers forever.
Model Council automatically runs GPT-5.4, Claude Opus 4.6, and Gemini 3.1 Pro in parallel, then synthesizes where they agree, disagree, and what each uniquely contributes.
OpenAI's latest GPT-5.4 models are now available to all Pro and Max subscribers, delivering gains in reasoning, coding, and creative tasks.
© Copyright 2026 Perplexity"},{"url":"https://www.aimagicx.com/blog/claude-opus-4-6-vs-gpt-5-4-vs-gemini-3-1-benchmark-comparison-april-2026","title":"Claude Opus 4.6 vs GPT-5.4 vs Gemini 3.1 Pro: The April 2026 Benchmark Breakdown | AI Magicx Blog | AI Magicx","content":"Grok 4 just posted a 75% on SWE-bench Verified, edging out GPT-5.4 at 74.9% and Claude Opus 4.6 at 74%.
Where the differences become more meaningful is on WebDev Arena and Aider Polyglot.
Claude Opus 4.6 scores 82.1% on WebDev Arena, a benchmark that tests full-stack web development tasks including HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and framework-specific patterns.
Multi-file refactoring — Claude Opus 4.6 — Aider Polyglot lead, best at coordinated changes across files
In production reasoning tasks -- analyzing complex business scenarios, processing legal documents, evaluating scientific papers -- Claude Opus 4.6 consistently produces more rigorous, more carefully qualified analysis.
Generally, smaller models are faster, and Gemini Flash leads on latency.
The frontier AI model landscape in April 2026 is the most competitive it has ever been."},{"url":"https://medium.com/@Micheal-Lanham/the-march-2026-frontier-gpt-5-4-vs-gemini-3-1-vs-claude-4-6-daebf22e672e","title":"The March 2026 Frontier: GPT-5.4 vs. Gemini 3.1 vs. Claude 4.6 | by Micheal Lanham | Medium","content":"Claude Opus 4.6 dropped February 5, followed. Gemini 3.1 Pro hit public preview on February 19. And GPT-5.4 landed March 5. Claude Opus 4.6 is ...
SWE-Bench Verified has been a go-to for coding comparisons, and Google’s table shows Gemini 3.1 Pro at 80.6%, Opus 4.6 at 80.8%, and Sonnet 4.6 at 79.6%, all clustered tight.
Google DeepMind reports MRCR v2 at 128K showing Gemini 3.1 Pro and Sonnet 4.6 tied at 84.9%.
On Arena.ai’s text leaderboard (March 5, 2026 snapshot), Claude Opus 4.6 sits at #1 (Elo 1504), Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview at #2 (1500, preliminary), and GPT-5.4-high appears at #7 (preliminary).
Abstract reasoning puzzles: Gemini 3.1 Pro leads on ARC-AGI-2 (77.1%), followed by GPT-5.4 (73.3%) and Opus 4.6 (68.8%).
Budget-conscious agentic work: Gemini 3.1 Pro at $0.32 per standard call versus $0.75 for Opus 4.6."},{"url":"https://www.mindstudio.ai/blog/gpt-54-vs-claude-opus-46-vs-gemini-31-pro-benchmarks","title":"GPT-5.4 vs Claude Opus 4.6 vs Gemini 3.1 Pro: Real Benchmark Results Compared | MindStudio","content":"HumanEval — 164 Python coding problems scored on pass@1
SWE-bench tests something more realistic: resolving actual GitHub issues.
The writing didn’t stand out, but it didn’t fail either.
It read more like strong commercial fiction than literary work.
Research synthesis where context fits within 200K tokens
Claude Opus 4.6 outperforms GPT-5.4 on creative writing quality, graduate-level reasoning (87.4% vs 83.9% on GPQA Diamond), and long-context document synthesis.
GPT-5.4 leads on coding accuracy (93.1% HumanEval, 52.7% SWE-bench) and complex SVG generation, and is the fastest of the three."},{"url":"https://mike.co.ke/ai/ai-trends-late-march-2026/","title":"AI Trends Late March 2026: GPT-5.4, Claude, Agents","content":"It also scores 78.3% on the MRCR v2 benchmark at 1 million tokens, the highest recall rate among frontier models as of this month.
Bottom line: Agents are not a future feature.
Gemini 3.1 Pro — Strong reasoning, 1M context, available via API and Vertex AI.
GPT-5.4 mini and nano — Released March 17."}]}
